## Summary
The Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) Year-1 data release presents baryon acoustic oscillation measurements from over 6 million extragalactic objects spanning redshifts 0.1 < z < 4.2. These represent the most precise BAO measurements to date across multiple tracer populations.

## Evidence
DESI measured the BAO feature using luminous red galaxies (0.4 < z < 0.8), emission line galaxies (0.8 < z < 1.6), quasars (0.8 < z < 2.1), and Lyman-α forest (1.77 < z < 4.16). Distance measurements achieve 0.5–2% precision per redshift bin. The transverse and line-of-sight BAO measurements are consistent with each other across all tracers.

## Significance
DESI Year-1 already surpasses previous surveys in statistical power. The hint of evolving dark energy from these measurements has generated intense theoretical interest and motivates urgent analysis of systematic uncertainties in photometric calibration and fiber assignment.
